description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the node:8.2.1 Docker image with a cache on node_modules/. Define three stages: setup, test, and deploy. In the setup stage add a setup-dist job that runs `npm install` then `npm run dist` and stores the generated `dist` directory as an artifact. In the test stage add a test job that runs `npm install` followed by `npm test`. In the deploy stage add four jobs: deploy-alpha (runs only on the alpha branch) that configures git user from ${NPM_EMAIL}/${NPM_USERNAME}, writes the npm auth token from ${NPM_TOKEN} to .npmrc, creates a pre‑release version with `npm version "$(node -p \"require('./package.json').version+'-'+require('uuid').v4()\")" -m "Patch release"` and publishes to npm with `npm publish --tag next`; deploy-major (manual, runs only on master) that configures git, writes .npmrc, bumps the major version with `npm version major` and publishes with `npm publish`; deploy-minor (manual, runs only on master) that does the same but bumps minor version and also pushes the commit with `git push origin master`; and deploy-patch (manual, runs only on master) that bumps the patch version and publishes. All deploy jobs target the npm registry.
